title: Static Accessibility Evidence, WCAG Mapping, and Review Handoff · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers static accessibility evidence, wcag mapping, and review handoff into a changed WCAG-linked static finding ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of WCAG-linked static finding ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty.
Audit document language, headings, landmarks, labels, instructions, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers as evidence, not templates.
Identify native semantics and attribute risks while noting accessible names, roles, values, and states may need browser computation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Image Purpose
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 4158 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Image Purpose.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Image Purpose, keep Image Purpose connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Conformance Boundary
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
